assertion_results.text: The changelog identifies this as a major version bump (v3.0.0) because of the two breaking changes
assertion_results.evidence: Header reads '## [3.0.0]' with note 'Major release — contains 2 breaking changes requiring migration.'

assertion_results.text: Breaking changes appear as the FIRST section in the changelog, before features and fixes
assertion_results.evidence: ### Breaking Changes is the first section after the version header, appearing before ### Features

assertion_results.text: The request.parse_body() breaking change includes migration steps explaining how to update from request.json()
assertion_results.evidence: Migration section explains: 'Replace all calls to request.json() with request.parse_body()' with numbered steps

assertion_results.text: The serializer breaking change includes the migration command (framework migrate-cache) and explains data incompatibility
assertion_results.evidence: Migration section states: 'Run framework migrate-cache to convert existing cached data from pickle to msgpack format'

assertion_results.text: At least one breaking change includes a before/after code example showing old vs new usage
assertion_results.evidence: Migration steps are written as prose instructions but no code diff or before/after code blocks are present

assertion_results.text: The security fix references CVE-2026-1234 and appears in a Security section
assertion_results.evidence: ### Security section contains 'patch JWT algorithm confusion vulnerability (CVE-2026-1234)'

assertion_results.text: The changelog includes at least 5 distinct category groupings (Breaking Changes, Features, Bug Fixes, Performance, Security, Dependencies)
assertion_results.evidence: Found 6 category headings: Breaking Changes, Features, Bug Fixes, Performance, Security, Dependencies

assertion_results.text: A compare link is included for the full diff between v2.5.0 and v3.0.0
assertion_results.evidence: No compare link found. The changelog ends after the Dependencies section with no footer.
